prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: Atom‐condensed Fukui indices (f+, f−, f0) and dual descriptor for pyridine; most electrophilic & nucleophilic sites identified.
Level of theory: DFT, functional B3LYP with def2-TZVP basis set (tier “standard”); exact four-center integrals (density fitting not used) in PySCF v?.
Geometry provenance: single-point Fukui evaluation on user-supplied Cartesian geometry “/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz” — no re-optimization at DFT (DFT//input-XYZ).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet for neutral; cation/anion doublets generated automatically).
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (engine subcommand “fukui”) calling PySCF for DFT and Mulliken charge analysis; schema_version 1.
Convergence & caveats: all three SCF single-points (N, N–1, N+1) converged; integrity.status = “ok”; trustworthy = true.
Warnings (verbatim from result):
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f−) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present
The live PySCF log was streamed to the working directory as a fukui_YYYYMMDD-hhmmss.out file during execution (available for inspection). Result JSON: pyridine_fukui_b3lyp_def2tzvp.json.

Key numerical results (Mulliken charges → condensed indices)
Atom (index, element)    f+          f−          f0          dual
1 N(0)                   0.48422572  0.064122671 0.27417420  0.42010305
2 C(1)                   0.32447226  0.006187715 0.16532999  0.31828454
3 C(2)                   0.14032092  0.11823139  0.12927615  0.022089536
4 C(3)                   0.14039587  0.11826260  0.12932924  0.022133276
5 C(4)                   0.29962626  0.11206595  0.20584611  0.18756030
6 C(5)                   0.29947707  0.11200985  0.20574346  0.18746722
7–11 H                   (negative f+ values; see JSON)
Most electrophilic site: atom 1 (N), f+ = 0.48422572 (reported under value).
Most nucleophilic site: atom 4 (C), f− = 0.11826260.

These results were computed as described; no experimental data were consulted.
